commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add automated install/deploy/uninstall shell scripts

install.sh — bare-metal Linux installer:
  - Detects distro (Ubuntu/Debian/RHEL/Fedora/Arch) and installs system
    deps (clang, llvm, libelf, protobuf-compiler, linux-headers)
  - Installs rustup + bpfel-unknown-none target + rust-src if missing
  - Clones repo or uses local source directory
  - Builds eBPF kernel program then userspace daemon
  - Installs binary to /usr/local/bin, config to /etc/vectorguard
  - Optionally starts Qdrant via Docker (--no-qdrant to skip)
  - Creates and enables a systemd service with correct capabilities
    (CAP_SYS_ADMIN, CAP_BPF, CAP_PERFMON, CAP_NET_ADMIN, CAP_SYS_PTRACE)
  - Options: --no-qdrant, --no-service, --config FILE

deploy-k8s.sh — Kubernetes deployer:
  - Auto-detects Helm or falls back to raw kubectl manifests
  - Supports --include-ns / --exclude-ns for namespace scope at deploy time
  - --adapter flag selects backend (tetragon|falco|auditd|native_ebpf)
  - --dry-run, --uninstall, --image for custom images
  - Waits for DaemonSet rollout and prints useful follow-up commands

uninstall.sh — bare-metal cleanup:
  - Stops and disables systemd service
  - Removes binary, systemd unit
  - Prompts before deleting config/rules (preserves user customizations)
  - Prompts before removing Qdrant Docker container + volume

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: map page — standard layout, correct design tokens, BottomNav

The /map page was using fixed inset-0 with its own standalone header,
hiding the standard layout and mobile navbar. Redesigned to match the
standard page pattern used by /tours:

- Removed fixed inset-0 fullscreen takeover
- Added standard glassmorphism sticky header (KH logo, theme toggle, profile)
- Added BottomNav with activePath="/map"
- Filter pills: text-black → text-premium-black, active glow shadow
- Filter counts: text-white/70 → text-premium-black/60 (readable on cyan)
- Map container: rounded-2xl with border-white/20, calc height
- Removed isExpanded/collapse state (unnecessary with standard layout)
- Added useTheme hook for theme toggle

Note: Map shows "API ключ не настроен" because NEXT_PUBLIC_YANDEX_MAPS_API_KEY
is empty in .env.local and on Timeweb Cloud. Need to set a valid Yandex Maps
API key to fix this.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(api): correct epoch lookup for staking activity events

Use sorted epoch list (descending by start_height) instead of random map
iteration. end_height is 0 for all epochs, so match by finding the first
epoch with start_height <= block_height.

fix(108): backfill duration and fix invitees for recordings (#112)

Three migrations addressing missing data on recordings (issue #108):

1. Backfill duration (20260309200000): 1,526 fathom recordings had NULL
   duration because the original bulk import skipped this field. Computes
   duration directly from recording_start_time/recording_end_time which were
   already populated. Fixes 1,526 of 1,532 NULL rows (remaining 6 are YouTube
   recordings with no end_time — need YouTube API re-fetch).

2. Fix fathom_calls view (20260309200001): CallDetailPanel queries invitees
   via fathom_calls view filtered by canonical_recording_id. The view was
   created before that column was added to fathom_raw_calls, so it silently
   omitted the column and all invitee lookups returned null. Recreating the
   view as SELECT * exposes the column and unblocks 1,547 recordings with
   existing calendar_invitees data.

3. Null bogus durations (20260309200002): Two recordings had corrupt
   end timestamps years in the future, producing durations of 120M+ seconds.
   Nulls out any duration > 12 hours (43200s).

Note: speakers are parsed client-side from full_transcript — no backend
change needed. YouTube recordings without duration require a separate
YouTube API re-fetch (tracked separately).

Closes #108

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix Date Night to check if business is open RIGHT NOW, not just today

isOpenToday only checked if the day had hours — a business with Monday
hours 9am-5pm would show at 12:05 AM Monday. Replaced with isOpenNow
which compares current time against open/close times, with proper
late-night wrap handling (e.g., 9pm-2am Sunday carries into Monday).

feat: Shared Tool Library — Phase 1 (helpers & constants) (#32)

* feat: extract shared tool library (Phase 1 — helpers & constants)

Move helper functions and constants from ai-chat Edge Function into
supabase/functions/_shared/ so any Edge Function can import them.
This is the architectural prerequisite for autonomous AI workflows
(Layer 3) which need the same business logic without the chat UI.

What moved to _shared/:
- constants.ts (caregiver + client phase definitions)
- helpers/phone.ts (normalizePhoneNumber)
- helpers/caregiver.ts (7 functions: detectPhase, resolveCaregiver, etc.)
- helpers/client.ts (6 functions: getClientPhase, resolveClient, etc.)
- helpers/ringcentral.ts (4 functions: getRingCentralAccessToken, etc.)

ai-chat helper files become re-export shims for backward compatibility.
All 8 tool files, registry, prompt, and context layer are unchanged.
ringcentral.ts reads Deno.env directly instead of importing from config.ts,
removing the dependency on ai-chat's config module.

Tests: 399/400 passing (1 pre-existing time-sensitive flake unrelated)
Build: clean

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: stabilize flaky actionEngine test (1.5d → 1.1d threshold)

The "interview not scheduled after 1 day" test used 1.5 days which
could drift past the 2-day critical threshold depending on time of
day. Changed to 1.1 days to stay safely in the warning range.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf(pipeline): O(1) ready-step tracking with incremental dependency resolution

Replace the O(V*E) GetReadySteps scan with an incrementally maintained
sorted ready-list. MarkExecuted now propagates completion to dependents
via reverse edges, decrementing remainingDeps counters and inserting
newly-ready steps in sorted order.

Key changes:
- DependencyGraph gains remainingDeps map and sorted ready slice
- addReady/removeReady maintain the ready list with binary search
- MarkExecuted is now idempotent (no-op on double-mark)
- ExecutedCount uses a counter instead of iterating the map
- New tests: out-of-order replay, idempotent marking
- New benchmark: 32-level x 16-wide DAG scheduling throughput
- restore.go: remove unused filepath import
- session_test.go: add encoding/json import for new test coverage

commit.message:
fix: ensure hook files exist for project-level install

Project-level install (--project) wrote settings.json with commands
referencing $HOME/.claude/hooks/ but never created the symlinks/copies
there, causing hooks to fail. Now both scopes create hook files first.

commit.message:
Patch built-in LinkSuggest instead of using separate EditorSuggest

Obsidian only shows one EditorSuggest at a time and the built-in link
suggester wins for [[ triggers. Instead, find the built-in LinkSuggest
instance and inject our alias matches directly into its getSuggestions,
so they appear naturally in the existing popup.

commit.message:
Add reference implementation — runnable 4-table Membase with web UI

Complete reference/ directory extracted from the Agent Red Knowledge Database:
- db.py: Core KnowledgeDB class (4 tables, append-only versioning, _UNSET sentinel)
- assertions.py: Machine-verifiable grep/glob/grep_absent assertion runner
- seed.py: Self-referential example data (7 specs, 2 procedures, 1 handoff)
- app.py: Flask read-only web dashboard (7 routes, dark theme)
- hooks/: SessionStart (assertion-check) + UserPromptSubmit (spec-classifier)
- templates/: 8 Jinja2 templates + static/style.css
- QUICKSTART.md: 5-minute bootstrap guide
- .gitignore: Python/SQLite/IDE ignores
- README.md: Updated with Quick Start section and reference/ entry

Audit and fix button variants across dialogs, settings, import, and pages
to ensure visual harmony per the brand button system (issue #106).

  CopyToOrganization, MoveToWorkspace, OrganizationInvite, WorkspaceManagement)
- Join pages (WorkspaceJoin, OrganizationJoin): Decline button ghost → hollow
  to create proper primary/secondary visual hierarchy
- TeamTab: Copy Link button outline → hollow to match secondary action standard
- WorkspaceManagement inline dialog: Cancel outline → hollow
- RoutingRuleSlideOver: Replace raw <button> elements with Button component
  (ghost/icon close button, hollow Cancel, default Save)
- RoutingRulesTab: Replace raw <button> elements with Button component
  (default Create Rule, hollow Preview, default Apply Now, link Learn more)

  also removes stale v1 token (text-ink-muted)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix index.css @theme color conflicts with tailwind.config.js

In Tailwind v4, the CSS @theme block generates the CSS variables that
utility classes actually use — not tailwind.config.js. The @theme had
stale/wrong values:
  - background: #020208 (too dark, near-black) → #080812
  - surface: #080716 (overly blue-saturated) → #0e0d1d
  - surface-dark: #0a0918 → #050510

These mismatches caused bg-surface to render as the wrong color everywhere,
including Scott (OutlierScout). Now all theme tokens are unified.

commit.message:
feat(calculators): add headstock/nut break angle calculator

New calculator for string break angle at the nut:
- headstock_break_angle.py: HeadstockBreakAngleInput/Result + calculate_headstock_break_angle()
- Supports angled headstocks (Gibson/PRS) and flat headstocks (Fender)
- Computes angle from headstock pitch, nut-tuner distance, tuner post height
- For flat headstocks, factors in string tree depression
- Optimal nut break angle: 5-10 degrees
- POST /api/neck/break-angle endpoint

Tests: test_headstock_break_angle.py (281 lines)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
